topic.what: First lattice-based Distributed Broadcast Encryption (DBE) achieving simultaneous adaptive security and fully optimal (succinct) parameters from falsifiable assumptions
topic.how: Introduces Equivocal Encryption System methodology with dual real/fake modes enabling dynamic ciphertext equivocation to bypass matrix programming barrier
topic.why_matters: Resolves long-standing gap between selective and adaptive security in lattice-based DBE; provides post-quantum broadcast encryption with constant-size keys/ciphertexts

characteristics.vs_baseline: Optimal (constant); first adaptive-secure with this property
characteristics.context: Fully succinct in ROM and standard model constructions

characteristics.value: O(poly(lambda)) in ROM; O(N) or O(N^2) in standard model
characteristics.context: ROM version has constant CRS; standard model requires N or N^2 growth

characteristics.vs_baseline: First to achieve adaptive + optimal simultaneously from lattices
characteristics.context: Previously only selective or inefficient adaptive possible

characteristics.vs_baseline: Same assumptions as prior selective schemes

not_for: Settings requiring small CRS in standard model

novelty: Equivocal Encryption System methodology for adaptive security in lattices
Bypasses matrix programming barrier via fake mode with auxiliary trapdoors
Programmable Pseudorandom Set Sampler (PPS) tool for standard model
First adaptive lattice-based DBE with optimal parameters in ROM
First adaptive lattice-based DBE in standard model from falsifiable assumptions
open_problems: Optimal CRS construction in standard model (currently O(N))
Generic applicability to other lattice-based encryption systems
Reducing CRS size while maintaining adaptive security
